combat_history.event: JS Maya and JS Haguro conducted tracking of multiple North Korean ballistic missile tests, including intermediate-range missiles that overflew Japan
combat_history.significance: First operational use of dual-band radar system in real-world BMD scenario, validating tracking capabilities

combat_history.event: JS Maya participated in trilateral missile defense exercises with US and South Korean forces, successfully tracking simulated ballistic missile targets
combat_history.significance: Demonstrated interoperability of Baseline 9 system with allied BMD networks

modernization.description: Integration of more capable SM-3 Block IIA interceptors to counter advanced ballistic missile threats including potential hypersonic glide vehicles
modernization.impact: Significantly extends BMD engagement envelope and improves kill probability against maneuvering targets

modernization.description: Integration of CEC to share radar tracks in real-time with other Aegis platforms
modernization.impact: Creates networked air defense umbrella, improves tracking of low-observable targets

modernization.description: Potential integration of Tomahawk land-attack cruise missiles in support of Japan's counterstrike capability policy
modernization.impact: Would transform from purely defensive to offensive-capable platform

vulnerabilities.description: Limited magazine depth with only 96 VLS cells creates vulnerability to large-scale coordinated missile attacks
vulnerabilities.context: China's growing inventory of anti-ship and land-attack cruise missiles could overwhelm defensive magazines
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Relies on networked defense with other platforms and land-based systems

vulnerabilities.description: Heavy reliance on radar systems makes the platform vulnerable to advanced electronic attack and cyber warfare
vulnerabilities.context: Chinese and Russian EW capabilities specifically target Aegis radar systems
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Upgraded ECM systems and dual-band radar provide some redundancy

vulnerabilities.description: Current SM-3 inventory may struggle against maneuvering hypersonic glide vehicles
vulnerabilities.context: China's DF-17 and similar weapons present new challenge to traditional BMD
vulnerabilities.mitigation: SM-3 Block IIA and future interceptors designed to address this threat

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: BMD patrol stations in Sea of Japan and Philippine Sea, integrated with USFJ BMD network
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: Operates independently or with Atago-class destroyers and US Aegis ships
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: High operational tempo due to frequent North Korean missile tests requiring BMD alert status

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Similar Aegis Baseline 9 but with 128 VLS cells and more balanced strike capability. Maya prioritizes BMD over offensive weapons due to Japanese constitutional constraints.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Allied Aegis showdown - different approaches to the same threat environment

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Larger (12,000t vs 8,200t) with 112 VLS cells and integrated mast design. Type 055 emphasizes offensive strike while Maya focuses on defensive BMD capability.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: David vs Goliath - Japan's defensive destroyer against China's offensive cruiser

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Maya incorporates newer Baseline 9 vs Flight IIA's Baseline 7. Similar VLS count but Maya has dual-band radar advantage and dedicated BMD mission focus.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Student vs teacher - how Japan improved on the original Aegis design

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Different approach with SAMPSON/S1850M radar vs Aegis. Type 45 optimized for air defense, Maya for BMD. Similar displacement but very different sensor philosophy.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: East meets West - competing air defense destroyer philosophies
